---
title: "DISTRIBUCIONES"
author: "Orlando Galarza"
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: true
    theme: flatly        
    highlight-style: tango
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: false
    df-print: paged
    smooth-scroll: true
    link-external-newwindow: true
    embed-resources: true
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#install.packages("readr")
```

```{r}
#install.packages("fitdistrplus")
```

```{r}
library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(fitdistrplus)
```

descargamos FAMOSOS

```{r}
fam<-read_csv("FAMOSOS1.xlsx - Hoja1 (7).csv")
fam
```

DISTRIBUCIÓN PARA CONTINUAS: ESTATURA:

```{r}
fdN<-fitdist(fam$Estatura, "norm")
fdN
fdE<-fitdist(fam$Estatura, "exp")
fdE
fdU<-fitdist(fam$Estatura, "unif")
fdU
```

```{r}
gofstat(list(fdN, fdE, fdU))
```

```{r}
plotdist(fam$Estatura, histo=TRUE, demp=TRUE)
```

Considerando que la estatura es normal, calcule

```{r}
media_real<-mean(fam$Estatura)
sd_real<-sd(fam$Estatura)
X1 <- 170
prob_mas_170 <-pnorm(X1, mean = media_real, sd = sd_real, lower.tail = FALSE)

cat("P(X > 170):", round(prob_mas_170, 4), "\n")

# Gráfico del caso
x_val <- seq(media_real - 4*sd_real, media_real + 4*sd_real, length = 1000)
df_norm <- data.frame(x = x_val, y = dnorm(x_val, media_real, sd_real))
df_norm

ggplot(df_norm, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2, color = "#2c3e50") +
  geom_area(data = subset(df_norm, x >= X1), aes(y=y), fill = "#e74c3c", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = X1, linetype = "dashed", color = "#c0392b") +
  annotate("text", x = X1 + 1, y = 0.10, 
           label = paste0("P(X > 170)\n= ", round(prob_mas_170 * 100, 2), "%"), 
           color = "#c0392b", fontface = "bold") +
  labs(title = "Probabilidad de estatura mayor a 170cms",
       subtitle = paste0("X ~ N(", round(media_real, 2), ", ", round(sd_real, 2), ")"),
       x = "Estaturas de famosos", y = "Densidad") +
  theme_minimal()
```
